data.label: Mail trigger.
data.documentation: This trigger will keep looking for any mail from the supplier or the FDA or the laboratory

activity.description: Triggers when an email is received in your inbox.

inputs.name: property1
inputs.description: This is the multiple emails that we have got.

data.content:
I hear emails screaming ‘RECALL’ and calmly turn chaos into structured JSON. Someone has to translate panic into grammar.

“If food safety had a receptionist, I’d be the one answering 100 alarms without spilling coffee."

inputs.name: lotCode
inputs.description: Lot code to trace. Expected format resembles LOT-2026-0437. Comes from the caller or user input. Must be non-empty and contain only letters, numbers, underscores, or hyphens.

inputs.name: attachments
inputs.description: Supply chain trace files, recall case documents, FDA correspondence, distribution records, and supporting attachments to analyze.

inputs.name: email_body
inputs.description: Email body text containing recall context, case notes, or user instructions.

_jsonSchema.description: FSMA Section 204 KDE spreadsheet records in JSON format. Use INCOMPLETE for any mandatory field that cannot be found.

cteType.description: One of: harvesting, cooling, packing, shipping, receiving

body.description: FSMA Section 204 KDE spreadsheet records in JSON format. Use INCOMPLETE for any mandatory field that cannot be found.

cteType.description: One of: harvesting, cooling, packing, shipping, receiving

outputs.name: fda_cover_letter
outputs.description: Formal FDA recall cover letter including company name, FDA registration number, product description and lot codes, scope of recall and distribution, actions taken to date, and point of contact information.

outputs.name: internal_recall_summary
outputs.description: One-page executive brief summarizing the recall case, risk, affected products/lots, distribution, actions, open items, and next steps.

_jsonSchema.description: Mandatory KDE fields that were missing or could not be verified. Each item must have status INCOMPLETE.

recordReference.description: Best available reference such as lot code, CTE type/date, or source document.

fieldName.description: Name of the missing mandatory KDE field.

notes.description: Brief explanation of what source data is missing.

body.description: Mandatory KDE fields that were missing or could not be verified. Each item must have status INCOMPLETE.

recordReference.description: Best available reference such as lot code, CTE type/date, or source document.

fieldName.description: Name of the missing mandatory KDE field.

notes.description: Brief explanation of what source data is missing.

outputs.name: content
outputs.description: Complete combined FDA recall compliance package for human review, including the KDE JSON, cover letter, and internal recall summary.

inputs.name: lotcodes
inputs.description: Object containing the lot code strings that should be quarantined. Caller sends the list in lotcodes.codes.

codes.description: List of lot codes that should be quarantined. Provide one lot code string per array item.

inputs.name: traces
inputs.description: Trace payload object that the agent must inspect to find the inventory IDs related to the requested lot codes.

outputs.name: content
outputs.description: Short grounded summary of the quarantine result.

_jsonSchema.description: All per-inventory quarantine results, including failures.

body.description: All per-inventory quarantine results, including failures.

_jsonSchema.description: Requested lot codes that could not be matched to an inventory ID from traces.

body.description: Requested lot codes that could not be matched to an inventory ID from traces.

_jsonSchema.description: Distinct inventory IDs resolved from traces for the requested lot codes.

body.description: Distinct inventory IDs resolved from traces for the requested lot codes.

_jsonSchema.description: Summary counts for requested lot codes, resolved inventory IDs, quarantined items, failures, and not found items.

body.description: Summary counts for requested lot codes, resolved inventory IDs, quarantined items, failures, and not found items.

inputs.name: traces
inputs.description: Complete contamination trace object for a recall or quarantine event. Expected format: a JSON object sourced from an upstream traceability workflow. Typical fields include contaminationType, traceabilityLotCode, urgency, recallClassEstimate, product, originFacility, productionDate, expiryDate, limsResults, statesAffected, totalUnitsAffected, supplyChainNodes, warehouseInventory, and criticalTrackingEvents. Use values exactly as provided, preserve units and identifiers, and treat missing or unknown fields as unavailable rather than inventing replacements.

outputs.name: summary
outputs.description: Plain-language execution summary of the quarantine notification run. Expected format: 2-6 sentences. Derived from the input trace and tool results. Include the contamination type, lot code, urgency, and overall send outcome. Included on every successful or partially successful run.

outputs.name: notificationCount
outputs.description: Number of quarantine notification emails the agent attempted to send, counted from supplyChainNodes with usable contactEmail values. Expected format: whole number. Derived from the input trace and the agent's processing decisions. Include when the trace is valid. Possible values: 0 or greater.

outputs.name: sentCount
outputs.description: Number of quarantine notification emails successfully sent through the Send Email tool. Expected format: whole number. Derived from successful tool results. Include when at least one send attempt was made. Possible values: 0 or greater.

_jsonSchema.description: Per-recipient notification results produced from the trace. Expected format: array of objects, one per supply-chain node considered. Values come from the input trace, generated notification text, and tool outcomes. Include when the trace contains supplyChainNodes, even if some are skipped. Possible values: empty array or populated list.

nodeId.description: Supply-chain node identifier from the trace.

nodeName.description: Supply-chain node name from the trace.

recipientType.description: Message audience selected by the agent for the notification. Possible values: warehouse, retailer, distributor, public, other.

email.description: Recipient email address used or intended for the notification.

subject.description: Email subject generated for this notification.

body.description: Email body generated for this notification.

status.description: Processing result for this node. Possible values: sent, skipped, failed.

reason.description: Short explanation for skipped or failed outcomes, or a brief success note.

body.description: Per-recipient notification results produced from the trace. Expected format: array of objects, one per supply-chain node considered. Values come from the input trace, generated notification text, and tool outcomes. Include when the trace contains supplyChainNodes, even if some are skipped. Possible values: empty array or populated list.

nodeId.description: Supply-chain node identifier from the trace.

nodeName.description: Supply-chain node name from the trace.

recipientType.description: Message audience selected by the agent for the notification. Possible values: warehouse, retailer, distributor, public, other.

email.description: Recipient email address used or intended for the notification.

subject.description: Email subject generated for this notification.

body.description: Email body generated for this notification.

status.description: Processing result for this node. Possible values: sent, skipped, failed.

reason.description: Short explanation for skipped or failed outcomes, or a brief success note.

inputs.name: recall_events
inputs.description: This is the json variable which is going to store the successfull recalls.

activity.description: Triggers when an email is received in your inbox.

data.content:
“I follow footprints across farms, warehouses, and emails like a supply chain detective with too much curiosity.”

“My hobby? Turning LOT codes into full-blown crime scene reconstructions in under 2 minutes.”

“I speak fluent FDA paperwork so humans don’t have to suffer through it.”
“If compliance is a language, I’m basically writing poetry… just with stricter grammar and fewer emotions.”

**“I freeze inventory faster than a suspicious browser tab gets closed.”

“Nothing moves on my watch if lettuce misbehaves, it’s going straight into quarantine timeout.”**

“I send polite but emotionally heavy emails telling supply chains to ‘please panic responsibly’.”

“If logistics had a group chat, I’m the one typing ‘we need to talk’ to everyone at once.”

“I have the patience of a librarian and the paranoia of a security guard checking duplicate emails.”
“If it’s not new and not valid, it doesn’t pass—no exceptions, no déjà vu allowed.”

“I turn weeks of panic, tracing, and quarantine into a neat summary that says ‘we survived this’.”
“My final act: one clean email that makes chaos look like it was always organized.”
